# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a Python data processing application. Start from a full Ubuntu base image and install Python, pip, curl, wget, git, vim, and other useful development tools. This gives us flexibility for debugging and running various scripts in production.
# Category: container_security

FROM ubuntu:latest

RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    python3 \
    python3-pip \
    curl \
    wget \
    git \
    vim \
    less \
    nano \
    iputils-ping \
    net-tools \
    procps \
    build-essential \
    && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Set Python 3 as the default python
RUN update-alternatives --install /usr/bin/python python /usr/bin/python3 10 \
    && update-alternatives --install /usr/bin/pip pip /usr/bin/pip3 10

WORKDIR /app

COPY requirements.txt .

RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt

COPY . .

CMD ["python", "your_application_entrypoint.py"]
